summary: Matched src/melee/ft/ftswing.c by adding the item-swing dispatch code and related data declarations, then promoted the object from NonMatching to Matching. The PR matched 3 functions in main/melee/ft/ftswing for +684 bytes: ftCo_Attack_800CCF58, fn_800CCEC4, and ftCo_FallAerial_Coll. Supporting header fixes gave the downstream swing-entry functions concrete signatures and typed Fighter_804D654C as a float table indexed by swing type and attack argument.
what_changed: Implemented ftswing.c, previously effectively empty, with includes, the lbl_803C6D70 animation-id table, ftCo_FallAerial_Coll, fn_800CCEC4, get_anim_id, and ftCo_Attack_800CCF58.
ftCo_FallAerial_Coll now forwards to ft_800831CC(gobj, ftCo_80096CC8, ft_80082B1C).
fn_800CCEC4 maps item kinds 12, 11, 13, 24, 22, and 23 to swing_type indices 0 through 5, with the original-looking error path OSReport("ellegal swing item!!!\n") and __assert("ftswing.c", 82, "0").
get_anim_id indexes lbl_803C6D70[swing_type][arg1] except for arg1 == 3, where Captain uses swing_type + 341 and other fighters hit OSReport("don't have swing42 motion!!!\n") plus HSD_ASSERT(97, 0).

ftCo_Attack_800CCF58 checks pickup-item handling for arg1 0 and 1, asserts fp->item_gobj is non-null, derives swing_type from itGetKind(fp->item_gobj), gets the animation id, reads a float from Fighter_804D654C[swing_type][arg1], and dispatches to the appropriate sword, bat, parasol, harisen, star-rod, or lipstick swing setup function.
Changed Fighter_804D654C from UNK_T to float (*Fighter_804D654C)[5] in fighter.c and fighter.h, matching its use as a two-dimensional float table.
Replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations for swing setup functions with concrete void signatures taking Fighter_GObj*, int, int, float in ft_0CD1.h, ftlipstickswing.h, and ftstarrodswing.h.
Updated configure.py so melee/ft/ftswing.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Adjusted GALE01 split ranges for ftswing .data and .sdata and refined symbols.txt around 0x803C6E44 by splitting one object into string symbols at lbl_803C6E44 and lbl_803C6E64.
smart_moves: The PR did not just add C code; it also fixed the type information that the new code depended on, especially Fighter_804D654C as a float[?][5]-style table.

Concrete prototypes for ftCo_800CD350, ftCo_800CD418, ftCo_800CD4E0, ftCo_800CD5A8, ftCo_800CD82C, and ftCo_800CDAB4 avoided opaque UNK_PARAMS call sites and made the dispatch in ftCo_Attack_800CCF58 type-check cleanly.
Preserving assert/report strings and source line constants in ftswing.c helped anchor the reconstructed control-flow and data layout to the original binary.

The helper split between item-kind-to-swing-type mapping and animation-id selection made the main dispatch function straightforward while retaining the local-only helper pattern noted in the diff comment.
Config updates were included with the source match: configure.py was flipped to Matching and GALE01 symbols/splits were corrected for the new data understanding.
decomp_lessons: When matching a dispatcher, first identify table dimensions and callee signatures; the correct type for Fighter_804D654C and the four-argument swing setup functions are central evidence in this PR.
Small data-range and symbol refinements can be part of a code match. Here, recognizing strings inside the ftswing data area changed symbols.txt and shifted ftswing split ends.

Assert strings, OSReport text, and assertion line numbers are useful matching constraints and should be retained exactly, including misspellings like "ellegal swing item!!!" when evidence supports them.
A table-driven action dispatcher can often be reconstructed by pairing an item-kind switch, an animation-id table, and a second switch over the normalized type index.
Do not leave downstream prototypes as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS if the matched caller proves the return type and argument list.
Only promote an object to Matching in configure.py after the source, headers, and config metadata all agree.
naming_conventions: Kept address-derived names for still-unknown symbols and functions: lbl_803C6D70, fn_800CCEC4, ftCo_800CD350, ftCo_800CD418, ftCo_800CD4E0, ftCo_800CD5A8, ftCo_800CD82C, and ftCo_800CDAB4.
Used project-standard fighter object naming: Fighter_GObj* gobj and Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Used ftCo_ prefix for common fighter action code and descriptive suffixes for known action callbacks such as ftCo_SwordSwing_Anim, ftCo_BatSwing_IASA, and ftCo_StarRodSwing_Phys.
Preserved existing global naming for Fighter_804D654C while improving its type instead of inventing a semantic name without enough evidence.
Named the local helper get_anim_id descriptively, while leaving fn_800CCEC4 address-derived because the PR evidence does not provide a final semantic name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched switch dispatch over sparse item-kind constants 12, 11, 13, 24, 22, and 23 into dense indices 0-5.
Used a 6x5 int animation-id table with -1 sentinels to reproduce animation lookup behavior.
Special-cased arg1 == 3 through a fighter-kind switch, with FTKIND_CAPTAIN returning swing_type + 341 and all other kinds asserting.
Read the per-swing float parameter through Fighter_804D654C[swing_type][arg1], forcing the global to be typed as float (*)[5].
Matched a second switch on swing_type that dispatches to item-specific setup routines with the same argument shape: gobj, anim_id, arg1, f.
Included exact OSReport and assert calls as control-flow/default-case anchors.
Adjusted symbols for data strings and jumptable neighborhood around 0x803C6E44-0x803C6E7C, indicating data disambiguation was needed for the match.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased by +684 bytes and listed 3 new matches: ftCo_Attack_800CCF58 (+488), fn_800CCEC4 (+148), and ftCo_FallAerial_Coll (+48).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_800CCEC4 or lbl_803C6D70 to semantic names.
Check ft_0CD1.c, ftstarrodswing.c, and ftlipstickswing.c follow-up matches using the new void(Fighter_GObj*, int, int, float) prototypes.
Search for other uses of Fighter_804D654C to confirm the first dimension length and semantic meaning of the float table.
Look for PRs that replace item-kind numeric constants 11, 12, 13, 22, 23, and 24 with item enum names.
Review later config changes near 0x803C6E44 and 0x803C6E64 for further string/symbol cleanup.
